output.title: Security Deposit Not Returned? Your Rights, Deductions & Demand Letter Guide
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to demand your money back fast.
content_outline: Understanding Why Landlords Withhold Security Deposits
What a Landlord Can Legally Deduct (State-by-State Rules)
How Long Does a Landlord Have to Return Your Deposit?
Writing an Effective Security Deposit Dispute Letter
Escalating the Dispute: Small Claims Court and Beyond
key_points: Most states limit security deposit deductions to unpaid rent, property damage beyond normal wear and tear, and excessive cleaning costs.
Landlords must return deposits within a legally defined timeframe—typically 14 to 45 days—or face statutory penalties.
A formal demand letter citing state law is the most effective first step to recover a withheld deposit without immediate litigation.
Tenants should compile move-in/move-out photos, lease agreements, and receipts to dispute wrongful deductions successfully.
output.cta: Stop waiting for your landlord to act. Use DepositBack to generate a legally sound, state-specific security deposit demand letter in minutes—and recover what’s yours.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware U.S. tenants who are frustrated because their landlord is withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and are actively searching for solutions to recover their money.
